back_translation: Are we beginning to introduce ourselves again? Or do we need letters of introduction to you or from you, like some people?
You yourselves are our letter, which is inscribed in our hearts, which all people have known and read.

It is clear that you are a letter from Christ, which we have served-to-completion, that has been inscribed not with ink, but with the Spirit of the living God, not on tablets of stone, but on tablets of hearts of flesh.
Now this kind of confidence we have toward God through Christ.
Not that we have suitable qualifications in ourselves to consider that anything is from us, but our suitability comes from God.
He has made us suitable to be servants of the new covenant, not of the letter, but of the Spirit; for the letter leads to death, but the Spirit gives life.

Now if the ministry of death which was inscribed with letters on tablets of stone came with glory, until the descendants of Israel could not stare at the face of Moses, because of the glory on his face that was fading away,
will not the ministry of the Spirit come with glory even more than that?
For if the ministry of judgment-and-condemnation has glory, the ministry of righteousness overflows with glory exceedingly more than that.
For indeed, what was once given glory in this respect is not considered to have glory at all, when compared with the surpassing greatness of glory beyond it.
For if what was fading away came with glory, what endures is even more abundantly with glory exceedingly.
Therefore, since we have such hope, we proclaim with great courage exceedingly.
And we are not like Moses, who used to put a cloth-cover over his face, so that the descendants of Israel would not stare at the end of what was fading away.

But the minds of them were hardened. For until this day, the same cloth-cover still remains during the reading of the old covenant, without being lifted, because only in Christ will the cloth-cover be removed.
But until this day, whenever Moses is read, there still remains a cloth-cover upon their heart.
But whenever the person turns back to the Lord, the cloth-cover is taken off.
Now the Lord is the Spirit, and wherever the Spirit of the Lord dwells, there is freedom.

And we all, with faces that have no cloth-cover, while we behold the glory of the Lord as if reflected in a mirror, are being transformed into the same image, from glory to glory, just as comes from the Lord who is the Spirit.
